description: Creates a new Opportunity record in Salesforce with validation and sharing rule enforcement. Requires two-step confirmation before execution. Enforces CRUD permissions and field-level security.

AccountId.description: Account ID (18 characters, required). Must be a valid Account the user has access to.

CloseDate.description: Expected close date in YYYY-MM-DD format (required)

StageName.description: Opportunity stage (required). Must be one of the allowed stages: Prospecting, Qualification, Proposal, or Negotiation.

examples: Renewal opportunity for existing enterprise customer
New business opportunity from inbound lead

salesforceUserId.description: Salesforce User ID (15 or 18 characters) for authorization enforcement. REQUIRED for security.

sessionId.description: Optional chat session ID for tracking multi-turn conversations

confirmationToken.description: Confirmation token generated during preview step. Required to prevent replay attacks.

outputValues.description: Output values from the Flow execution

requestId.description: Unique request ID for tracking and support

inputs.Name: ACME Renewal - Enterprise License
inputs.Description: Annual renewal for enterprise license. Customer expressed interest in expanding to additional business units.

inputs.Name: New Business - Q1 2026
inputs.Description: New business opportunity from inbound marketing lead. Customer is evaluating our platform for their sales team.

output.error: You've reached the daily limit of 20 for this action. Try again tomorrow.

whenToUse: When the user explicitly requests to create a new Opportunity
When the user provides sufficient information to populate required fields (Name, Account, CloseDate, Stage)
When the user wants to track a new sales opportunity or deal
As a follow-up action after searching for an Account
bestPractices: Always show a preview of the proposed Opportunity before executing
Require explicit user confirmation via the two-step confirmation flow
Validate that the AccountId exists and the user has access to it
Use reasonable defaults for optional fields (e.g., Amount, Description)
Provide clear feedback about what will be created
Include the created Opportunity ID in the success message with a deep link
limitations: Maximum 20 opportunities can be created per user per day (configurable via ActionEnablement metadata)
User must have Create permission on Opportunity object
User must have access to the specified Account
StageName must be a valid value from the org's Opportunity Stage picklist
Action can be disabled by administrators via ActionEnablement metadata
confirmationFlow: 1. Agent proposes creating an Opportunity with specific field values
2. LWC displays preview modal showing all fields that will be populated
4. On confirm, LWC generates confirmation token and calls /action endpoint
5. Action Lambda validates token, checks rate limits, and executes Flow
6. LWC displays success message with link to created Opportunity

securityNotes.authorization: This action enforces Salesforce CRUD permissions and sharing rules. Users can only create Opportunities if they have Create permission on the Opportunity object and access to the specified Account.
securityNotes.confirmation: Two-step confirmation is REQUIRED. The action will fail if confirmationToken is missing or invalid.
securityNotes.rateLimiting: Rate limits are enforced per user per day. Default limit is 20 creates per day, configurable via ActionEnablement metadata.
securityNotes.auditLogging: All action executions are logged to AI_Action_Audit__c custom object with user ID, inputs (hashed if PII detected), success status, and timing metrics.
securityNotes.dataPrivacy: All communication occurs over private networking (PrivateLink). No data traverses the public internet.
securityNotes.killSwitch: Administrators can disable this action immediately by setting Enabled__c=false in ActionEnablement metadata.

general: Always show preview before executing actions
Only suggest this action when user explicitly requests to create an Opportunity
Gather all required information (Name, Account, CloseDate, Stage) before proposing the action
If user doesn't provide an Account, search for it first using retrieve_knowledge tool
Use natural language to describe what will be created in the preview
examplePrompts: Create an opportunity for ACME worth $500K closing next quarter
I want to create a new opportunity for the renewal
Add an opportunity to track this deal
Create a $1M opportunity for Account XYZ
responseFormat: When proposing the action, say: 'I can create an Opportunity with the following details: [list fields]. Would you like me to proceed?'
After successful creation, say: 'I've created the Opportunity "[Name]" for [Account Name]. You can view it here: [link]'
If rate limited, say: 'You've reached the daily limit for creating Opportunities. You can create more tomorrow.'
If validation fails, say: 'I need more information to create the Opportunity: [missing fields]'
NOTE: Due to confirmationToken requirement, this action currently requires the LWC component (ascendixAiSearch) to execute. Direct Agentforce invocation will fail until a wrapper is implemented.
